channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Join ADB Challenges: Turning Innovative Ideas into Impactful Solutions in Asia-Pacific
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
ADB Challenges is more than just a program—it is a global movement that brings together innovators, entrepreneurs, students, and organizations to transform bold ideas into practical solutions that address real-world challenges across Asia and the Pacific. Through this platform, problem solvers collaborate, experiment, and develop innovative approaches to tackle pressing development issues. Participants have the […]

The post Join ADB Challenges: Turning Innovative Ideas into Impactful Solutions in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
ADB Challenges is more than just a program—it is a global movement that brings together innovators, entrepreneurs, students, and organizations to transform bold ideas into practical solutions that address real-world challenges across Asia and the Pacific.

Through this platform, problem solvers collaborate, experiment, and develop innovative approaches to tackle pressing development issues. Participants have the opportunity to showcase their creativity, refine their solutions, and potentially contribute to impactful projects that improve lives and strengthen communities in the region.

Whether you are a student, startup founder, researcher, or company representative, ADB Challenges provides a unique platform to collaborate with like-minded innovators and turn ideas into action.

Explore the Latest Challenges

Participants are encouraged to explore the newest challenges available on the platform and identify opportunities that align with their expertise or interests. Each challenge focuses on solving specific problems and invites creative, practical solutions from individuals and teams worldwide.

How to Participate in an ADB Challenge

Joining an ADB Challenge is a straightforward process. Participants simply need to follow these steps:

1. Submit a Solution

If you find a challenge that interests you or your organization, click the Participate button and submit your proposed solution. This is your chance to present innovative ideas that could help solve real-world problems.

2. Deliverables

Once you enter a challenge, select the relevant category and begin working on the required deliverables. Make sure to carefully follow the challenge guidelines and submit your work before the deadline.

3. Selection Process

After the application phase closes, the selection committee reviews all submitted deliverables. Participants should allow time for the evaluation process and stay updated on announcements.

Note: Some challenges may include multiple rounds of selection where applicants will be asked to refine or improve their submissions.

4. Final Event

Shortlisted participants will receive further instructions to prepare for the final pitch event. This stage provides an opportunity to present your solution to judges and challenge administrators.

If you have questions during the process, participants are encouraged to contact the respective challenge administrators for guidance.

ADB Challenges in Numbers

Since its launch in 2018, ADB Challenges has built an impressive track record of engagement and innovation:

  • 79 Challenges Hosted

  • 8,400+ Problem Solvers Engaged

  • 2,500+ Solutions Submitted

These figures reflect participation across all ADB Challenges since the program began.

Join the Movement

ADB Challenges continues to bring together creative minds from around the world to solve some of the region’s most pressing development issues. By participating, you can collaborate with global innovators, refine your ideas, and contribute to meaningful change.

Together, let’s create, innovate, and build solutions that shape a better future for Asia and the Pacific.

Follow the platform for updates on upcoming challenges, success stories, and new opportunities to participate.

Discover more similar details here.

The post Join ADB Challenges: Turning Innovative Ideas into Impactful Solutions in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Nominations: $10,000 Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application 2026
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application, endowed by The Rockefeller Foundation, is now accepting nominations for outstanding young professionals transforming agriculture and food systems around the world. Presented annually by the World Food Prize Foundation, the award recognizes exceptional science-based achievements in international agriculture and food production by individuals under the age […]

The post Call for Nominations: $10,000 Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application, endowed by The Rockefeller Foundation, is now accepting nominations for outstanding young professionals transforming agriculture and food systems around the world.

Presented annually by the World Food Prize Foundation, the award recognizes exceptional science-based achievements in international agriculture and food production by individuals under the age of 40. The winner receives a $10,000 prize and global recognition during World Food Prize Week in Des Moines.

The award honors individuals working directly “in the field” with farmers, fishers, herders, and rural communities to advance sustainable food production and combat hunger and poverty.

Application Deadline: June 1

About the Norman Borlaug Field Award

Named after Nobel Peace Prize laureate Norman Borlaug, the award celebrates early-career leaders whose field-based work improves agricultural productivity, food availability, and nutrition globally.

Candidates are recognized for demonstrating courage, determination, and innovation while working directly with rural communities across the food production, processing, and distribution chain.

Award Criteria

Nominees are evaluated based on achievements reflecting Dr. Borlaug’s legacy, including:

1. Persistence
Demonstrating determination and resilience in addressing agricultural challenges despite adversity.

2. Innovation
Developing creative solutions and new approaches to ensure global food security and nutrition.

3. Communication
Working effectively across cultures and disciplines while engaging communities and stakeholders.

4. Research and Science
Applying rigorous scientific methods to improve food production, distribution, and nutrition.

5. Extension
Bringing innovations in plant, animal, or food science to underserved farmers and communities.

6. Education
Providing practical training and scientific education to rural stakeholders.

7. Application
Implementing improved technologies or management systems that support sustainable agriculture and reduce poverty.

8. Leadership
Guiding collaborative agricultural initiatives and inspiring professionals working to fight hunger.

9. Impact
Increasing food availability and improving livelihoods through measurable outcomes.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application, nominees must:

  • Be under the age of 40 (must not have reached their 40th birthday before World Food Day, October 16 of the award year).

  • Be actively working in the research, project, or discipline for which they are nominated.

  • Be affiliated with a public or private educational, research, or development organization.

Additional notes:

  • Nominees remain eligible for future consideration if they continue to meet the age and award criteria.

  • The award is typically presented to one individual, though rare collaborative cases may allow a shared award.

Nomination Requirements

All nominations must be submitted online in English through the official nomination portal.

Required documents include:

1. Nomination Statement (up to 3,000 words)
This should describe:

  • The nominee’s work and accomplishments

  • How their work reflects Dr. Borlaug’s early career attributes

  • The measurable impact of their contributions

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) or Resume

Must include:

  • Date of birth

  • Country of origin

  • Educational background

  • Professional experience

3. Letters of Support

  • One nomination letter

  • Two supporting letters

These should clearly highlight the nominee’s achievements and alignment with the award criteria.

4. Photos

  • One high-resolution headshot (minimum 300 dpi)

  • Up to two additional action photos showing the nominee at work

Who Can Submit a Nomination?

  • Any individual or organization may nominate a candidate.

  • Organizational nominations must be signed by an executive officer.

  • Self-nominations are not permitted.

Nominators should have direct knowledge of the nominee’s work and provide clear examples demonstrating the nominee’s impact.

Selection Process

An independent jury of international agricultural experts appointed by the World Food Prize Foundation reviews all nominations.

The jury is chaired by Ronnie Coffman, a member of the World Food Prize Council of Advisors who previously worked with Norman Borlaug in Mexico.

Note: The Rockefeller Foundation does not participate in the selection process.

Award Timeline

  • Nomination Deadline: June 1

  • Winner Announcement: August–September

  • Award Ceremony: During World Food Prize Week around World Food Day (October 16) in Des Moines

Award Benefits

The selected winner will receive:

  • $10,000 cash prize

  • Global recognition at the World Food Prize Week ceremony

  • Round-trip airfare to Des Moines

  • Accommodation and meals during the event

  • Opportunities to participate in media engagements and international discussions on food security

How to Submit a Nomination

Nominators must complete the official submission form.

Discover more similar Opportunities here.

The post Call for Nominations: $10,000 Norman Borlaug Award for Field Research and Application 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo 2026 for Students and Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Students and early-career professionals looking to gain valuable public sector experience are invited to attend the NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo 2026. This in-person event offers a unique opportunity to connect directly with representatives from multiple New York City agencies that are recruiting for summer internship positions. Organized by the NYC Department of Citywide […]

The post Apply Now: NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo 2026 for Students and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and early-career professionals looking to gain valuable public sector experience are invited to attend the NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo 2026. This in-person event offers a unique opportunity to connect directly with representatives from multiple New York City agencies that are recruiting for summer internship positions.

Organized by the NYC Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS) through its Office of Citywide Recruitment, the expo aims to help aspiring professionals kick-start their careers while serving the people of New York City.

Event Overview

The internship hiring expo will bring together representatives from more than 80 city agencies, all looking to recruit talented students and young professionals for summer internship opportunities.

Participants will have the chance to interact with recruiters, learn about available internship roles, and potentially interview on the spot. These internships provide practical experience in public service while helping participants develop professional skills and build networks across city government.

DCAS encourages attendees to register for only one session—either morning or afternoon—to allow more candidates to participate.

Event Details

  • Event: NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo

  • Date: March 26, 2026

  • Time: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM

  • Duration: 4 hours

  • Format: In-person

Venue:
David N. Dinkins Manhattan Municipal Building
1 Centre Street
New York, NY 10007
United States

Why Attend?

Attending the NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo offers several benefits:

  • Direct access to recruiters from over 80 New York City agencies

  • Potential on-site interviews for summer internship positions

  • Hands-on experience in public service working with city government

  • Career development opportunities for students and early-career professionals

  • Networking opportunities with professionals across multiple departments

Participants will also gain insights into how city agencies operate and how their work contributes to improving the lives of New Yorkers.

Accessibility Support

If you require a reasonable accommodation to attend the event, you should contact the DCAS Workforce Operations team at SIP@dcas.nyc.gov no later than March 19, 2026 to make arrangements.

About the Organizer

The event is organized by the NYC Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS) Office of Citywide Recruitment. DCAS supports the City of New York by managing recruitment, civil service administration, and workforce development across city agencies.

Through initiatives like this hiring expo, DCAS helps connect talented individuals with meaningful career opportunities in public service.

The NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo is an excellent opportunity for students and young professionals who want to gain real-world experience in city government. With representatives from dozens of agencies in attendance, participants can explore diverse career paths and take the first step toward a purposeful career serving New York City.

Interested candidates are encouraged to register early and prepare for a day of networking, learning, and career discovery.

For more details about the expo, please click here. 

Discover more similar opportunities  here. 

The post Apply Now: NYC DCAS Summer Internship Hiring Expo 2026 for Students and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Smithsonian Latino Museum Futures Internship 2026 in Washington, D.C. (Paid $550 Weekly Stipend + Housing)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Latino Museum Futures Internship (LMFI) is a prestigious professional development opportunity offered by the Smithsonian Institution through the National Museum of the American Latino. This internship program is designed to prepare emerging professionals for careers in museums and cultural institutions by providing hands-on experience, mentorship, and specialized training within one of the world’s most […]

The post Smithsonian Latino Museum Futures Internship 2026 in Washington, D.C. (Paid $550 Weekly Stipend + Housing) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Latino Museum Futures Internship (LMFI) is a prestigious professional development opportunity offered by the Smithsonian Institution through the National Museum of the American Latino. This internship program is designed to prepare emerging professionals for careers in museums and cultural institutions by providing hands-on experience, mentorship, and specialized training within one of the world’s most respected museum networks.

Hosted in Washington, D.C., the program allows selected participants to work closely with experts from the Smithsonian and partner institutions on real-world museum projects while gaining exposure to the professional museum environment. Participants also join a seminar series that deepens their understanding of museum practices, research, and exhibition development.

The Smithsonian is widely recognized as the largest museum, education, and research complex in the world, consisting of numerous museums, research facilities, and cultural institutions dedicated to knowledge sharing and preservation.

Through initiatives such as the Latino Museum Futures Internship, the Smithsonian aims to develop a diverse and highly skilled museum workforce capable of advancing the preservation and interpretation of cultural heritage.

About the Latino Museum Futures Internship (LMFI)

The Latino Museum Futures Internship was created to help train the next generation of museum professionals and prepare them for leadership roles in cultural institutions.

Participants take part in:

  • A structured museum seminar series

  • Practical museum-based projects

  • Mentorship by experienced Smithsonian staff

  • Networking opportunities with museum professionals

  • Hands-on training in museum operations

Interns gain experience working on real museum initiatives, which allows them to build professional portfolios and gain skills necessary for careers in museums and cultural heritage organizations.

Internship Fields and Project Areas

Participants in the Latino Museum Futures Internship work on projects that fall within one or more of the following museum fields:

1. Museum Curation – Interns may assist curators with research, artifact interpretation, and the development of museum exhibitions.

2. Collections Management – This area involves managing museum collections, cataloging artifacts, and ensuring proper preservation of historical objects.

3. Museum Conservation – Participants learn techniques used to preserve historical objects, artworks, and cultural artifacts.

4. Museum Education and Interpretation – Interns may work on programs designed to educate museum visitors through tours, workshops, or interactive exhibits.

5. Exhibition Design and Production – Participants can gain experience in designing exhibitions and creating engaging museum displays.

6. Digital Humanities and Emerging Technologies – Some projects explore the use of digital technology to present historical narratives and museum collections.

These experiences give interns exposure to multiple aspects of museum work, helping them identify potential career paths in the field.

Financial Benefits and Program Support

One of the major advantages of this internship is that it provides financial and logistical support to help participants complete the program successfully.

Selected interns receive the following benefits:

1. Weekly Stipend – Participants receive $550 per week to cover meals and local transportation expenses during the program.

2. Housing – The program provides shared housing in Washington, D.C. for the full duration of the internship.

3. Travel Support – Round-trip travel to Washington, D.C. is covered for participants traveling from within the United States, including Puerto Rico.

4. Professional Development – Interns benefit from:

  • Mentorship by Smithsonian experts

  • Career networking opportunities

  • Museum professional seminars

  • Real-world project experience

This combination of financial support and professional training makes LMFI one of the most valuable museum internships in the United States.

Program Timeline

The 2026 cycle of the Latino Museum Futures Internship follows the schedule below:

  • Applications Open: March 5, 2026

  • Application Deadline: April 20, 2026

  • Selection Notifications: June 2026

  • Program Start Date: August 31, 2026

  • Program End Date: November 20, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to ensure all materials are reviewed on time.

Who Can Apply

The Latino Museum Futures Internship targets students and emerging professionals interested in museum careers.

Eligible applicants generally include:

  • Undergraduate students

  • Graduate students

  • Early-career professionals interested in museum work

The program welcomes applicants from all academic disciplines, not only museum studies. Students in fields such as history, anthropology, art, digital humanities, education, and cultural studies may benefit from the experience.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The internship is primarily designed for individuals who are:

  • Studying in the United States

  • Authorized to work in the United States

International students may also apply if they are enrolled in U.S. institutions and have legal work authorization.

Applicants from any nationality may participate provided they meet these conditions.

Academic and Professional Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in museum work and cultural heritage.

Typical qualifications include:

Educational Background

Applicants are generally expected to be enrolled in or recently connected to an academic program such as:

  • Museum Studies

  • History

  • Art History

  • Anthropology

  • Cultural Studies

  • Education

  • Digital Humanities

Academic Interest

Applicants should show interest in areas such as:

  • Latino cultural history

  • Museum collections

  • Exhibition development

  • Cultural storytelling

Communication Skills

Strong written and verbal communication skills are highly valued.

Professional Motivation

Applicants must demonstrate enthusiasm for working in museums or cultural heritage institutions.

Application Requirements

Although the exact requirements may vary slightly each year, applicants typically need to submit the following materials:

Resume or Curriculum Vitae

A professional resume detailing academic achievements, internships, and relevant experience.

Personal Statements or Essays

Applicants are usually required to explain:

  • Their interest in museum careers

  • Their academic goals

  • Their motivation for joining the program

Supporting Documents

Depending on the project, applicants may also need:

  • Academic transcripts

  • References or recommendation letters

  • Portfolio (for design or digital projects)

Applications must be submitted through the Smithsonian’s official academic appointment system.

Apply here

Why This Internship Matters

The Latino Museum Futures Internship plays a critical role in developing the next generation of museum professionals and expanding diversity within the museum field.

Participants gain exposure to:

  • Smithsonian museum operations

  • Cultural preservation

  • Exhibition development

  • Academic research in museum studies

This program also contributes to the broader mission of documenting and preserving the history, art, and cultural contributions of Latino communities.

Career Opportunities After the Program

Completing the Latino Museum Futures Internship can open doors to careers in:

  • Museum curation

  • Cultural heritage preservation

  • Exhibition design

  • Archival research

  • Museum education

  • Digital humanities

Many former participants continue their careers in museums, universities, archives, and cultural organizations around the world.

Deadline

20th April 2026 (11:59 PM EST)

The Latino Museum Futures Internship offered by the Smithsonian provides an extraordinary opportunity for students and emerging professionals to gain hands-on experience in one of the world’s most respected museum institutions. With a weekly stipend, housing support, mentorship from museum experts, and real-world museum projects, the program is an excellent pathway into careers in cultural heritage and museum studies.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Smithsonian Latino Museum Futures Internship 2026 in Washington, D.C. (Paid $550 Weekly Stipend + Housing) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize 2027: Global Call for Nominations for One of the World’s Largest Humanitarian Awards
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize is one of the most prestigious international humanitarian awards recognizing organizations that have made extraordinary contributions toward alleviating human suffering. Established by the Conrad N. Hilton Foundation, the Prize celebrates nonprofit organizations that demonstrate exceptional leadership, innovation, and impact in addressing global humanitarian challenges. Each year, hundreds of organizations […]

The post Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize 2027: Global Call for Nominations for One of the World’s Largest Humanitarian Awards appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize is one of the most prestigious international humanitarian awards recognizing organizations that have made extraordinary contributions toward alleviating human suffering.

Established by the Conrad N. Hilton Foundation, the Prize celebrates nonprofit organizations that demonstrate exceptional leadership, innovation, and impact in addressing global humanitarian challenges.

Each year, hundreds of organizations from around the world are nominated for this prestigious award. After a rigorous selection process conducted by an independent international jury, one organization is selected as the laureate.

The Prize is widely recognized as one of the world’s largest humanitarian awards for nonprofit organizations, honoring those that have created meaningful and lasting impact in communities across the globe.

Organizations addressing issues such as poverty, health, disaster response, education, displacement, food insecurity, and social inequality are encouraged to be nominated.

About the Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize

The Hilton Humanitarian Prize recognizes nonprofit organizations that demonstrate:

  • Exceptional contributions to reducing human suffering

  • Strong track records of impactful humanitarian work

  • Innovative and sustainable program approaches

  • Effective partnerships and collaborations

  • Organizational strength and accountability

The Prize highlights organizations that not only address urgent humanitarian needs but also create sustainable solutions that transform lives.

Each year, more than 300 organizations worldwide are nominated, reflecting the global reach and prestige of the award.

Why the Hilton Humanitarian Prize Matters

The Hilton Humanitarian Prize is more than recognition. It elevates the visibility of organizations that are making meaningful contributions to humanity.

Receiving the Prize can:

  • Amplify global awareness of the organization’s mission

  • Strengthen credibility and partnerships

  • Increase funding opportunities

  • Expand humanitarian impact worldwide

Through this prestigious recognition, the Conrad N. Hilton Foundation continues to highlight organizations that are leading the fight against human suffering and driving sustainable change around the world.

Current Nomination Cycle

Nominations are accepted year-round, but the nomination date determines which Prize cycle the organization will be considered for.

Current nomination timeline:

2027 Hilton Humanitarian Prize
Submission period: May 1, 2025 – April 30, 2026

Deadline: April 30, 2026

Organizations nominated within this timeframe will be considered for the 2027 Hilton Humanitarian Prize.

Who Can Be Nominated

The Hilton Humanitarian Prize is awarded to organizations rather than individuals.

Eligible nominees must meet the following criteria:

  1. Must be a nonprofit organization.

  2. Must be an established nongovernmental and publicly supported charitable organization.

  3. Must possess tax-exempt status such as:

    • U.S. 501(c)(3), or

    • International equivalent documentation.

  4. Must have been legally established for at least five years.

  5. Must have audited financial statements for at least five consecutive years.

  6. Must have operational expenditures greater than USD 750,000 during the most recent audited fiscal year.

  7. Must not be:

    • A previous Hilton Humanitarian Prize laureate

    • A local office or branch of a previous laureate

    • A program or project within a larger organization

    • A university or academic institution that grants degrees

However, organizations affiliated with academic institutions may qualify if they:

  • Maintain independent nonprofit status

  • Have their own audited financial statements

Who Can Submit a Nomination

The nomination must be submitted by an individual who has direct knowledge of the organization’s work.

The nominator must meet the following conditions:

  • Must not be an employee or officer of the nominated organization

  • Must not receive compensation from the nominated organization

  • Must not be the founder of the organization

  • Must not be a family member of the organization’s employees or officers

  • Must not be a member of the Hilton Foundation staff, jury, or board

  • Must not be a Hilton family member

Board members and volunteers may nominate an organization only if they receive no compensation for their services.

Additionally, each nominator may submit only one nomination per Prize cycle.

Nomination Letter Requirements

The nomination letter is one of the most important components of the submission.

Key requirements include:

  • Must be written in English

  • Must be submitted through the online nomination portal

  • Must include a handwritten or electronic signature

  • Must not exceed four typed pages

  • Must use 12-point font

The letter should be addressed to:

Hilton Humanitarian Prize Jury

The nomination letter should focus on the organization’s achievements rather than future plans.

Key areas that should be addressed include:

  • The organization’s extraordinary contributions to alleviating human suffering

  • The significance of the humanitarian issues the organization addresses

  • The organization’s proven track record of impactful work

  • The strength and innovation of its programs

  • The sustainability and scalability of its approach

  • Partnerships and collaborations that enhance impact

  • Evaluation and learning mechanisms within programs

  • The potential impact the Prize would have on the organization’s mission

Evaluation Criteria

All nominations undergo a thorough review process based on several evaluation considerations.

These include:

  1. Extraordinary contributions toward alleviating human suffering.

  2. Importance and urgency of the humanitarian issues addressed.

  3. Demonstrated record of achievements and measurable impact.

  4. Strength of the organization’s programmatic strategy.

  5. Organizational capacity and operational effectiveness.

  6. Evidence of partnerships that enhance program reach and impact.

  7. Commitment to program evaluation and continuous improvement.

  8. Potential impact of the Prize on the organization’s work and mission.

Nomination and Selection Process

The Hilton Humanitarian Prize selection process is rigorous and multi-staged.

Step 1: Nomination Submission
The nominator submits the nomination through the official nominations portal and uploads the signed nomination letter.

Step 2: Nominee Notification
The Conrad N. Hilton Foundation contacts the nominated organization and invites them to submit additional information.

Step 3: Phase 1 Application
The nominated organization completes the Phase 1 application within approximately five business days.

Phase 1 includes:

  • Organizational profile

  • Mission and program descriptions

  • Communities served

  • Geographic scope of operations

  • Financial information

  • Audited financial statements

  • Annual reports or newsletters

Step 4: Internal Review
The Foundation conducts an internal review of all Phase 1 submissions.

Step 5: Phase 2 Application
Selected organizations are invited to complete a more detailed Phase 2 application. They are given approximately ten business days to submit additional materials.

Step 6: Finalist Selection
Following the Phase 2 review, a shortlist of finalist organizations is selected.

Step 7: Jury Review
Finalists are reviewed by an independent international jury.

Step 8: Prize Recipient Selection
The jury selects the final recipient of the Hilton Humanitarian Prize.

Timeline of the Selection Process

The general timeline is as follows:

  1. Nomination submission before the deadline

  2. Nominee notification via email

  3. Phase 1 application submission

  4. Phase 1 review

  5. Phase 2 application invitation

  6. Phase 2 review

  7. Shortlist of finalists

  8. Jury review

  9. Final Prize recipient selected

All nominators are notified of the final decision via email.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What do I need to do to submit a nomination?

To submit a nomination, visit the Hilton Humanitarian Prize nominations portal and upload the required nomination form and signed nomination letter before the deadline.

Email submissions are not accepted.

Can I nominate an individual for the Prize?

No. The Prize is awarded only to nonprofit organizations. Individual nominations are not accepted.

Can I nominate myself or my own organization?

No. Self-nominations are not allowed. The nomination must be submitted by an external nominator.

Can I nominate an organization where I serve as a board member?

Yes. Board members and volunteers may nominate an organization if they receive no compensation for their service.

How many organizations can I nominate in one cycle?

A nominator may submit only one nomination per Prize cycle.

However, it is possible to nominate two different organizations within the same year if they fall under different Prize cycles.

What happens if multiple people nominate the same organization?

Only the first nomination received will serve as the official nomination. Additional nominations will be registered as letters of support.

A maximum of four letters of support will be recorded.

How will organizations know they have been nominated?

The Hilton Foundation will contact the nominated organization directly via email using the contact information provided by the nominator.

Do organizations need to work internationally to qualify?

No. Organizations may operate in a single country or across multiple countries.

Are current or past Hilton Foundation grantees eligible?

Yes. Both current and past grantees are eligible to be nominated.

Read more and apply through the Official Website.

Discover other global awards on OFY HERE.

The post Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize 2027: Global Call for Nominations for One of the World’s Largest Humanitarian Awards appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ETH Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027 at ETH Zurich: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Early-Career Researchers
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme (ETH Fellows) at ETH Zurich is one of Europe’s most prestigious early-career research opportunities designed to support outstanding postdoctoral researchers from around the world. The fellowship enables talented scholars to conduct independent research while working with leading experts at ETH Zurich, one of the world’s top-ranked universities. This highly competitive […]

The post ETH Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027 at ETH Zurich: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme (ETH Fellows) at ETH Zurich is one of Europe’s most prestigious early-career research opportunities designed to support outstanding postdoctoral researchers from around the world. The fellowship enables talented scholars to conduct independent research while working with leading experts at ETH Zurich, one of the world’s top-ranked universities.

This highly competitive programme is specifically designed to foster academic independence and support researchers who have demonstrated exceptional scientific achievements early in their careers. Many past ETH Fellows have gone on to secure professorships and influential academic positions globally, with approximately 40–50% of fellows becoming professors within a few years of completing the fellowship.

The ETH Fellows programme provides full salary funding, research support, and access to world-class research infrastructure in Switzerland. Through mentorship from ETH Zurich faculty members, fellows gain the resources and collaborative environment necessary to develop cutting-edge research projects and accelerate their academic careers.

About ETH Zurich

ETH Zurich (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich) is internationally recognized for excellence in science, engineering, mathematics, and technology. The institution consistently ranks among the top universities globally and has produced numerous Nobel laureates, pioneering researchers, and influential innovators.

The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship programme contributes to ETH Zurich’s mission of advancing scientific discovery by attracting exceptional researchers from diverse backgrounds worldwide.

Key Objectives of the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme

The programme aims to:

  1. Support outstanding early-career researchers with strong scientific track records.

  2. Encourage innovative and independent research projects.

  3. Foster collaboration between international researchers and ETH Zurich faculty.

  4. Promote academic leadership and prepare fellows for future professorships.

  5. Strengthen global research networks across disciplines.

Fellowship Duration

The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship has a fixed duration of two years.

Important notes:

  • The fellowship cannot be extended beyond two years.

  • Fellows must start their fellowship within eight months after the submission deadline.

  • The fellowship start date must be the first day of a month.

A later start is only permitted in exceptional cases and requires approval from the ETH Zurich Grants Office.

Funding and Financial Support

ETH Fellows receive a comprehensive funding package that includes salary and research support.

Salary

The fellowship covers 100% of the fellow’s salary based on ETH Zurich standards.

Salary structure for 2026:

Year 1

  • Gross salary: CHF 92,500

  • Total employment cost: CHF 106,400

Year 2

  • Gross salary: CHF 97,200

  • Total employment cost: CHF 111,800

Total funding over two years:

  • Gross salary: CHF 189,700

  • Total employment cost: CHF 218,200

The total employment cost includes 15% social security contributions paid by ETH Zurich.

Research Funding

In addition to the salary, fellows receive:

  • CHF 12,000 per year for research expenses

This funding can be used for:

  • Research materials

  • Travel and mobility expenses

  • Conference participation

  • Scientific collaboration activities

Note: Research funds cannot be used for salary costs, and salary funds cannot be redirected toward research expenses.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet several strict eligibility criteria at the time of the submission deadline.

Doctoral Degree Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD degree, OR

  • Complete their PhD within six months after the submission deadline.

For applicants who have not yet completed their PhD:

  • A certification confirming the planned defense date must be submitted.

  • The document must be signed by the doctoral advisor or department chair.

PhD Time Limit

Applicants who already hold a PhD must have received the degree no more than two years before the submission deadline.

The decisive date is the official examination or defense date (viva voce).

Publication Requirement

Applicants must have:

  • At least one scientific publication in a peer-reviewed journal, OR

  • Received a prize for their doctoral thesis.

Mobility Requirements

The fellowship follows strict international mobility rules.

Applicants are not eligible if they:

  • Have resided or worked in Switzerland for more than 12 months in the three years before the deadline.

  • Have worked or studied for more than six months in the past three years at any of the following institutions:

    • ETH Zurich

    • EPF Lausanne

    • ETH Domain Research Institutes (PSI, WSL, Empa, Eawag)

    • University of Zurich

Additionally:

  • Applicants who received their PhD from ETH Zurich or the University of Zurich are not eligible.

Mentor Requirement

Each applicant must have a host professor at ETH Zurich who agrees to act as a mentor.

Important conditions:

  • A professor may support only one candidate per evaluation round.

  • The mentor must provide an official invitation letter.

  • The host professor must upload required documents in the application system.

Applicants are strongly advised to contact potential mentors well before the application deadline.

Project Proposal Expectations

Applicants are expected to propose their own original research project.

The programme prioritizes:

  • Independent research ideas

  • Innovative and impactful proposals

  • Projects demonstrating clear synergy with the host research group

Applicants and mentors should not have closely collaborated before the fellowship.

Reference Letters

Applicants must submit two external reference letters.

Requirements include:

  • Letters must be sent directly to the ETH Zurich Grants Office.

  • Submission must occur at least 10 days before the deadline.

  • Letters must include:

    • Institutional letterhead

    • Signature of the referee.

Application Deadlines

The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship programme has two submission deadlines each year.

2026 deadlines:

  • 2 March 2026, 17:00 Swiss local time

  • 1 September 2026, 17:00 Swiss local time

If a deadline falls on a weekend, it will be moved to the following Monday at the same time.

Interview Process

The selection process includes two stages:

  1. Written evaluation of the submitted proposal

  2. Interview for shortlisted candidates

The interviews are conducted online via video call.

For the 2026 calls:

March 2026 deadline interviews:

  • 19 May 2026

  • 20 May 2026

  • 21 May 2026

September 2026 deadline interviews:

  • 10 November 2026

  • 11 November 2026

  • 12 November 2026

Applicants are informed within two months after the deadline whether they have been invited for an interview.

Final decisions are announced approximately four months after the submission deadline.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted electronically through the ETH Zurich eResearch grant application system.

Steps to apply:

  1. Download the Official project description templates.

  2. Prepare the required documents and convert them into PDF format.

  3. Register an account on the eResearch platform.

  4. Start a new application under “My Applications”.

  5. Select the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship call.

  6. Complete all mandatory fields in the application form.

  7. Upload required documents.

  8. Add the host professor under the Participants section.

  9. Add two reference providers under the References section.

  10. Validate the application to ensure all required sections are complete.

  11. Submit the application before the deadline.

Once submitted, changes are no longer allowed.

Applicants are advised to download a PDF copy of the application for personal records.

Evaluation and Selection

Applications are evaluated by the ETH Zurich Research Commission.

Evaluation criteria include:

  • Scientific excellence

  • Research originality

  • Feasibility of the research proposal

  • Candidate’s academic track record

  • Alignment with the host research group

Due to the programme’s prestige and limited fellowships, the competition is intense.

Recent success rates have been approximately 25%.

Why Apply for the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship

This fellowship provides a unique opportunity for early-career researchers to:

  • Conduct independent research at a world-leading university

  • Collaborate with internationally recognized scholars

  • Gain access to cutting-edge research facilities

  • Build strong academic networks

  • Enhance career prospects in academia

  • Secure funding for innovative research projects

Many fellows successfully transition into tenure-track and professorship positions globally, making the programme a powerful launchpad for academic careers.

Click HERE to learn more about the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities HERE.

The post ETH Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027 at ETH Zurich: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NRF Call for Researchers Proposals for Funding in 2026–2027 – Now Open!
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Researchers, academic institutions, and research organizations now have the opportunity to apply for funding through the National Research Foundation (NRF) Call for Proposals for Funding in 2026 and 2027. The call supports a wide range of research activities, capacity-building initiatives, and collaboration programs designed to strengthen the research ecosystem and promote scientific innovation. The NRF, […]

The post NRF Call for Researchers Proposals for Funding in 2026–2027 – Now Open! appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers, academic institutions, and research organizations now have the opportunity to apply for funding through the National Research Foundation (NRF) Call for Proposals for Funding in 2026 and 2027. The call supports a wide range of research activities, capacity-building initiatives, and collaboration programs designed to strengthen the research ecosystem and promote scientific innovation.

The NRF, a key research funding agency in South Africa, provides financial support for projects that advance knowledge, build research capacity, and support emerging scholars across multiple disciplines. Through this funding call, the foundation aims to support researchers at different career stages while promoting international collaboration and research excellence.

Applicants are encouraged to review the relevant funding guides and submit proposals through the NRF’s online application system.

About the NRF Funding Calls

The NRF funding calls provide a structured platform for researchers and institutions to access funding across a variety of programs. These programs support different types of research activities including basic research, capacity development, international collaboration, and knowledge exchange.

The 2026–2027 call includes multiple funding instruments designed to support researchers at various career stages, from emerging scholars to established researchers. Applicants can access detailed application and funding guides that explain eligibility requirements, funding conditions, and evaluation criteria.

Applications must be submitted electronically through the NRF Connect system, which is the official platform used to manage submissions and evaluate proposals.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Research funding plays a critical role in supporting scientific discovery and innovation. By providing financial support for research projects, training programs, and international collaborations, the NRF helps strengthen the research ecosystem while enabling scholars to pursue new ideas and solutions.

Funding programs such as those included in the 2026–2027 call contribute to building research capacity, promoting academic collaboration, and addressing complex societal challenges through scientific research.

For researchers seeking funding to advance their projects or develop new research initiatives, the NRF call represents an important opportunity to secure support and expand their academic impact.

Types of Funding Opportunities Available

The NRF call includes several funding categories that support research development and academic collaboration. These opportunities are designed to strengthen the national and international research landscape while supporting both established and emerging researchers.

Research Grants

Research grants are available to support academic projects that contribute to scientific knowledge and innovation. These grants often fund research activities such as fieldwork, data collection, laboratory research, and collaborative research initiatives.

Several targeted programs are included in this category, including initiatives that support emerging researchers and promote research development.

Targeted Grants

Targeted grants focus on supporting specific groups or priority areas within the research community. One example is funding designed to support scholars participating in national academic development initiatives.

These grants help strengthen research capacity and promote the development of researchers who are advancing their academic careers.

Postdoctoral Grants

The NRF also offers funding opportunities for postdoctoral researchers, enabling recent PhD graduates to continue developing their research expertise. These grants support postdoctoral fellows who want to conduct independent research projects or collaborate with established research groups.

Postdoctoral funding is an important component of the NRF’s strategy to strengthen the pipeline of future academic leaders.

Travel, Training, and Conference Grants

Researchers can also apply for funding that supports international collaboration, training opportunities, and participation in academic conferences.

These grants may support activities such as:

  • Travel to international research institutions
  • Training programs or workshops
  • Participation in scientific conferences
  • Collaborative knowledge exchange initiatives

These opportunities help researchers expand their professional networks while sharing knowledge across institutions and countries.

Application Process

Applicants interested in the NRF funding opportunities must submit their proposals through the NRF Connect online system. The platform allows researchers to upload proposals, supporting documents, and institutional approvals required for submission.

Researchers are advised to review the General Application Guide and the relevant funding frameworks before submitting their proposals. These documents outline important information such as:

  • Eligibility criteria
  • Application requirements
  • Evaluation and review processes
  • Funding conditions and reporting requirements

Institutions may also set internal submission deadlines that occur before the official NRF closing dates, so applicants should coordinate with their institutional research offices when preparing their applications.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 April 2025

Applicants should carefully review the relevant funding guides and submit their proposals before the official deadline listed in the NRF application portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post NRF Call for Researchers Proposals for Funding in 2026–2027 – Now Open! appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Visiting Assistant Professor of Africana Studies Position at Brown University (2026–2027)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Scholars specializing in Africana Studies or related fields have an exciting academic opportunity to join one of the world’s leading research institutions. The Department of Africana Studies at Brown University is currently inviting applications for a Visiting Assistant Professor of Africana Studies for the 2026–2027 academic year. This full-time academic position offers the opportunity to […]

The post Apply for the Visiting Assistant Professor of Africana Studies Position at Brown University (2026–2027) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Scholars specializing in Africana Studies or related fields have an exciting academic opportunity to join one of the world’s leading research institutions. The Department of Africana Studies at Brown University is currently inviting applications for a Visiting Assistant Professor of Africana Studies for the 2026–2027 academic year. This full-time academic position offers the opportunity to contribute to a vibrant intellectual community while teaching and conducting interdisciplinary research.

Located in Providence, Rhode Island, Brown University is one of the United States’ prestigious Ivy League institutions, known for its commitment to innovative teaching, interdisciplinary scholarship, and inclusive academic environments. Founded in 1764, the university hosts a diverse community of scholars working across a wide range of disciplines.

The Visiting Assistant Professor will join Brown’s highly respected Africana Studies department, which is recognized for its strengths in areas such as history, gender and sexuality studies, political theory, public policy, and interdisciplinary Black studies.

What the Position Offers

This visiting faculty role provides scholars the opportunity to teach, conduct research, and contribute to the intellectual life of the department during a one-year academic appointment.

The selected candidate will teach four courses during the academic year, typically two courses per semester. These courses will primarily serve undergraduate students, although there may be an opportunity to teach a graduate seminar depending on departmental needs and the candidate’s expertise.

Beyond teaching, the visiting assistant professor will also:

  • Maintain an active research or creative scholarship agenda
  • Participate in departmental events, workshops, and academic discussions
  • Contribute to the broader intellectual community within Africana Studies
  • Engage with students through mentoring and academic guidance

The department values faculty members who bring intellectual curiosity, innovative teaching methods, and interdisciplinary approaches to the study of Africa and the African diaspora.

Areas of Scholarly Interest

While the area of specialization for this role is open, the department is particularly interested in candidates whose work aligns with emerging and interdisciplinary approaches within Africana Studies.

Examples of areas of interest include:

Arts, Culture, and Aesthetics

  • Visual art and curatorial practices
  • Film, media studies, and literature
  • Fashion, material culture, music, and sound studies

Technology, Science, and the Environment

  • Environmental justice and climate change
  • Black technocultures and extractive industries
  • Science and technology studies related to African communities

Political Economy and Public Life

  • Public policy and governance
  • Urban studies and social welfare
  • Health equity and political economy

Scholars whose research and teaching extend beyond traditional historical approaches and embrace interdisciplinary perspectives are especially encouraged to apply.

Salary and Benefits

The Visiting Assistant Professor role offers an annual salary of $70,000 for the academic year.

For international scholars requiring immigration sponsorship, Brown University provides J-1 visa sponsorship for this position, allowing qualified candidates from around the world to participate in the appointment.

Working at Brown also provides access to extensive academic resources including research libraries, interdisciplinary institutes, and collaborative research centers that support faculty scholarship and teaching.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following core requirements:

  • A Ph.D. in Africana Studies, African Studies, or a related field completed or expected by the start of the appointment
  • Demonstrated solo teaching experience at the college or university level beyond teaching assistant roles
  • Evidence of interdisciplinary scholarship or creative engagement

Preferred candidates may also have experience in innovative course design or mentoring diverse student populations.

Application Materials

Applicants must submit the following documents through the Interfolio application system:

  1. Cover letter describing qualifications, teaching philosophy, and research focus
  2. Current curriculum vitae (CV)
  3. Teaching statement including course descriptions and two sample syllabi
  4. Writing sample or creative portfolio (maximum 25 pages)
  5. Contact information for three references

These materials allow the search committee to evaluate each candidate’s teaching experience, research trajectory, and potential contribution to the department.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: April 5, 2026

Review of applications will begin on March 4, 2026 and will continue until the position is filled. Candidates are encouraged to apply early to receive full consideration.

To read the full job description and apply, visit the Interfolio platform.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the Visiting Assistant Professor of Africana Studies Position at Brown University (2026–2027) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for EU Funding: Up to €2.25 Million for Digital Innovation and AI Health Research in Africa
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Organizations working at the intersection of digital innovation, artificial intelligence, and public health now have the opportunity to secure significant funding through a new European Union call under Horizon Europe. The Global Health EDCTP3 Joint Undertaking has launched a funding opportunity focused on advancing digital transformation in health research across Sub-Saharan Africa. The call invites […]

The post Apply for EU Funding: Up to €2.25 Million for Digital Innovation and AI Health Research in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working at the intersection of digital innovation, artificial intelligence, and public health now have the opportunity to secure significant funding through a new European Union call under Horizon Europe. The Global Health EDCTP3 Joint Undertaking has launched a funding opportunity focused on advancing digital transformation in health research across Sub-Saharan Africa.

The call invites international research consortia to develop projects that apply digital technologies, data systems, and artificial intelligence (AI) to strengthen clinical research capacity, improve healthcare delivery, and support innovative health solutions across the region.

By supporting collaborative projects between African and European institutions, the initiative aims to help build sustainable digital health ecosystems that can address major public health challenges while accelerating scientific innovation.

Organizations with expertise in health research, digital technology, data systems, and public health partnerships are encouraged to submit proposals.

About the Funding Opportunity

This funding call is part of the Global Health EDCTP3 program, an initiative designed to strengthen global health research partnerships between Europe and countries in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The program focuses on advancing medical research, improving health systems, and promoting collaboration between international institutions to address pressing health challenges.

Through this specific call, the European Union aims to support projects that explore how digital innovation and AI-driven technologies can enhance research systems and healthcare delivery in African contexts.

Digital transformation is becoming an increasingly important tool in global health. From data platforms that track disease trends to artificial intelligence models that support clinical decision-making, digital tools can help researchers and healthcare providers respond more quickly and effectively to emerging health challenges.

By funding projects that combine technology, research capacity building, and international collaboration, the program aims to create scalable solutions that can strengthen health systems across multiple countries.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Digital technologies are playing an increasingly important role in improving healthcare systems worldwide. From data analytics and artificial intelligence to mobile health platforms and digital research infrastructure, these tools can significantly enhance how health services are delivered and how research is conducted.

However, many health systems still face barriers to implementing digital innovation at scale, including limited infrastructure, funding constraints, and gaps in technical expertise.

This funding call aims to address those challenges by supporting collaborative research projects that combine digital innovation, international expertise, and capacity building.

By investing in digital health research partnerships between Africa and Europe, the initiative seeks to accelerate the development of solutions that improve health outcomes while strengthening scientific collaboration across regions.

Funding and Project Scope

Selected projects can receive up to €2.25 million in funding, making this one of the more significant digital health research opportunities currently available through the Horizon Europe framework.

Key funding details include:

  • Maximum funding per project: Up to €2.25 million
  • Total budget available: €18 million
  • Estimated number of projects funded: Approximately 8
  • Funding mechanism: Horizon Europe grant under a Coordination and Support Action

The funding is designed to support collaborative research initiatives that promote innovation while strengthening research infrastructure and partnerships.

Projects should not only develop new digital solutions but also demonstrate how these tools can be applied effectively in real healthcare systems.

Priority Areas for Proposals

The funding call invites projects that focus on one or more of the following areas:

1. Digital Innovation and Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare
Projects may explore how AI-driven technologies can support diagnostics, treatment decision-making, health data analysis, or healthcare management systems.

2. Digital Tools for Clinical Research and Health Services
Proposals can focus on integrating digital tools and data platforms into clinical research systems or health service delivery.

3. Research Capacity Strengthening
Another priority is building stronger research infrastructure and expertise in digital health and data science across Sub-Saharan Africa.

4. Cross-Border Collaboration and Knowledge Exchange
Projects are encouraged to promote partnerships between institutions across different countries to strengthen research networks.

5. Scaling Digital Health Solutions
The program also supports initiatives that can expand or scale proven digital health innovations across multiple regions.

By focusing on these areas, the initiative seeks to ensure that digital health innovations can move beyond pilot programs and deliver real benefits to healthcare systems and communities.

Who Can Apply

Applications must be submitted by international consortia made up of multiple partner organizations.

Eligible partners may include:

  • Universities and academic research institutions
  • Hospitals and clinical research centers
  • Public health organizations
  • Technology companies and digital innovation organizations
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

Each consortium must include at least three independent organizations from different countries, and at least one of these partners must be based in Sub-Saharan Africa.

This structure encourages collaboration between African and European institutions while ensuring that projects are grounded in local expertise and regional health priorities.

Geographic Focus

Projects funded under this call must focus on Sub-Saharan Africa, with partnerships involving organizations from:

  • African countries
  • European Union Member States
  • Countries associated with the Horizon Europe program

By supporting partnerships between institutions across these regions, the program aims to create long-term collaborations that strengthen global health research and digital innovation capacity.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: September 1, 2026

Because proposals require the formation of international research consortia, applicants are encouraged to begin identifying potential partners well in advance of the deadline. You learn more about the requirements and apply on the European Commission website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for EU Funding: Up to €2.25 Million for Digital Innovation and AI Health Research in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Dell NextGen Presales Academy 2026 in Amsterdam: Launch Your Tech Career with Dell Technologies
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the NextGen Presales Academy at Dell Technologies, a unique training program designed to help recent graduates start their careers in the technology industry. The program is based in Amsterdam, Netherlands, and offers participants the opportunity to develop the skills needed to become Inside Presales Solution Architects. Through structured training and […]

The post Dell NextGen Presales Academy 2026 in Amsterdam: Launch Your Tech Career with Dell Technologies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the NextGen Presales Academy at Dell Technologies, a unique training program designed to help recent graduates start their careers in the technology industry.

The program is based in Amsterdam, Netherlands, and offers participants the opportunity to develop the skills needed to become Inside Presales Solution Architects. Through structured training and hands-on learning, the program prepares graduates to work as trusted technical advisors in one of the world’s leading technology companies.

About the Dell NextGen Presales Academy

The NextGen Presales Academy is an intensive professional development program that runs for 12 to 24 months. It is designed to transform talented graduates into highly skilled presales professionals who can bridge the gap between technology solutions and customer needs.

Participants will receive training on Dell’s technology solutions, customer engagement strategies, and technical consulting practices. The program also emphasizes developing communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills that are essential for working with clients in complex IT environments.

What Participants Will Learn

During the academy, participants will gain practical knowledge and industry experience through structured training modules and real-world projects. The program focuses on developing expertise in:

Technology consulting and presales strategies
Understanding enterprise IT infrastructure and solutions
Customer engagement and relationship management
Technical presentation and communication skills
Problem-solving and analytical thinking in technology environments

By the end of the program, graduates will be equipped with the knowledge and confidence to support customers in selecting and implementing technology solutions.

Who Should Apply

The NextGen Presales Academy is designed for recent graduates who have a passion for technology and want to build a career in technical consulting or solution architecture.

Ideal candidates are individuals who are curious about emerging technology trends and enjoy solving complex problems while working closely with customers and business teams.

Requirements

Applicants should meet the following criteria:

A degree in a technical or business-related field
Strong interest in IT and technology trends
Excellent communication and relationship-building skills
An analytical mindset with a strong drive to achieve results

Candidates who demonstrate curiosity, adaptability, and a willingness to learn will thrive in this program.

Why This Program Matters

Programs like the NextGen Presales Academy provide an important pathway for young professionals to enter the technology industry. Rather than requiring years of experience, the program focuses on developing talent through training, mentorship, and hands-on practice.

Participants gain exposure to real-world business challenges while working with experienced professionals, helping them build a strong foundation for long-term careers in technology consulting and enterprise solutions.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can learn more and submit their applications through the official application portal.

For more job opportunities from OFY, Click here.

The post Dell NextGen Presales Academy 2026 in Amsterdam: Launch Your Tech Career with Dell Technologies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
